pattern: An interval game is solved by computing each player's best achievable score margin over the opponent on every subarray.
hints: Think in terms of the score difference (current player minus opponent) rather than two separate totals.
For a subarray, the player picks an end and then faces the opponent on the smaller subarray, so the value is the picked number minus the opponent's best on the rest.
Fill a table dp[i][j] = best difference on nums[i..j]; the answer is whether dp over the whole array is non-negative.

edge_inputs.description: single element, player one takes everything
player one cannot win this one
player one wins via the large middle value
two equal elements is a tie, counts as a win
two unequal, second is larger but player one picks first
all equal even length is a tie

stress.description: small random arrays within the tiny length bound
